hypothesis: Extending the PCDiff framework to jointly generate composition vectors and mesoscale microstructure descriptors (e.g., fiber orientation, filler dispersion, and percolation pathways) under physics-informed constraints (non-negativity, sum-to-one, manufacturability, and percolation feasibility) will yield a diffusion model that achieves higher predictive accuracy for multi-objective properties (yield strength and thermal conductivity) and greater design-space validity and coverage under realistic fabrication variability than composition-only diffusion.
